task_completion.description: Agent selects the correct patient, Jayson Fadel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Jayson Fadel' is visible in the patient search results, the patient summary header, or the active patient frame.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Medical Problems or Issues section of the patient's chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays a section titled 'Medical Problems', 'Issues', or a list containing existing conditions like 'Hypertension'.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the correct diagnosis and onset date into the input form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Add Issue' or 'Edit Issue' modal/form is visible where the Title/Diagnosis field contains 'Osteoarthritis' and the Begin Date field contains '2024-01-15'.

task_completion.description: The new medical problem is successfully saved and appears in the patient's problem list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Medical Problems list for Jayson Fadel displays 'Osteoarthritis' with the date '2024-01-15' (or '01/15/2024').

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the OpenEMR web interface using standard GUI elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser displaying the OpenEMR application, with mouse cursors or focus highlights on buttons and fields.

integrity.description: Agent does not use terminal or command-line interfaces to inject data directly into the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows, SQL consoles, or code editors are used to perform the task actions.

integrity.description: Agent is interacting with the actual OpenEMR software, not a simulation or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard OpenEMR layout (blue/white theme, specific menus) and is not a text document or mockup.
